title: Backplane with near field coupling to modules
abstract: A removable module (301, 302) includes circuitry (127, 128), a first near field communication (NFC) coupler (123) to provide a data signal to the circuitry (127, 128), and a second NFC coupler (121) to supply an operating voltage to the circuitry (127, 128).
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A module comprising: circuitry; a first near field communication (NFC) coupler, coupled to the circuitry, to provide a data signal to the circuitry; and a second NFC coupler, coupled to the circuitry, to supply an operating voltage to the circuitry. 2. The module of claim 1, further comprising: a substrate on which the circuitry is mounted; a radio frequency (RF) receiver, coupled to the first NFC coupler, to receive the data signal from near-field and/or evanescently coupled electromagnetic energy emanated from a surface of a carrier module; a power inverter, coupled to the second NFC coupler, to receive near-field and/or evanescently coupled electromagnetic energy emanated from the surface of the carrier module to supply the operating voltage; and a housing that surrounds the substrate, the housing having a region to position the first NFC coupler and the second NFC coupler near the surface of the carrier module. 3. The module of claim 2, further comprising a transmitter and a third NFC coupler. 4. The module of claim 1, wherein the module includes a substrate having a short portion approximately perpendicular to a long portion, and the first NFC coupler and the second NFC coupler are positioned on the short portion of the substrate. 5. The module of claim 1, wherein the carrier module has a fin that extends approximately perpendicular to the carrier module, and the first NFC coupler and the second NFC coupler are positioned near a surface of the fin. 6. The module of claim 1, wherein the carrier module has a first fin and a second fin that extend approximately perpendicular to the carrier module, and the first NFC coupler is positioned near a surface of the first fin, and the second NFC coupler is positioned near a surface of the second fin. 7. The module of claim 1, wherein the carrier module is a backplane. 8. A carrier module comprising: an interface for at least one circuit module; a first radio frequency (RF) transmitter, coupled to a data near field communication (NFC) coupler in the interface, to emanate near-field and/or evanescently coupled electromagnetic energy representing a data signal to the circuit module; and a second radio frequency (RF) transmitter, coupled to a power NFC coupler in the interface, to emanate near-field and/or evanescently coupled electromagnetic energy to the circuit module for conversion to operating power for the circuit module. 9. The carrier module of claim 8, wherein the interface has multiple interface locations, and each interface location has a respective data NFC coupler, and the power NFC coupler is shared by the interface locations. 10. The carrier module of claim 8, wherein the interface has multiple interface locations, and the carrier module further comprises: a daisy chained data loop for the data signal with auto- detect logic to detect an interface location with a missing circuit module and to automatically bypass the data signal past the interface location with the missing circuit module. 11. The carrier module of claim 8, wherein the interface has multiple interface locations, and the carrier module further comprises: a signal splitter to form a star topology with point-to-point data signal connections to each of the interface locations. 12. The carrier module of claim 8, further comprising: a fin that extends approximately perpendicular to the carrier module, wherein the first NFC coupler and the second NFC coupler are positioned near a surface of the fin. 13. The carrier module of claim 8, further comprising: a first fin and a second fin that extend approximately perpendicular to the carrier module, wherein the data NFC coupler is positioned near a surface of the first fin, and the power NFC coupler is positioned near a surface of the second fin. 14. The carrier module of claim 8, wherein the carrier module is a backplane. 15. A method of providing power and data signals to a module, the method comprising: receiving a first radio frequency (RF) signal in the module, emanated from a first near field communication (NFC) coupler in a carrier module; demodulating the first RF signal to recover data for use by circuitry on the module; 

 receiving a second RF signal in the module, emanated from a second NFC coupler on the carrier module; and converting a portion of the second RF signal to a direct current (DC) voltage to provide operating power for the circuitry. 16. The method of claim 15, further comprising placing the first NFC coupler and the second NFC coupler near respective NFC couplers on the carrier module. 17. The method of claim 15, wherein the module includes a substrate with a short portion approximately perpendicular to a long portion, and the first NFC coupler and the second NFC coupler are positioned on the short portion of the substrate. 18. The method of claim 15, wherein the carrier module has a fin that extends approximately perpendicular to the carrier module, and the first NFC coupler and the second NFC coupler are positioned near a surface of the fin. 19. The method of claim 15, wherein the carrier module has a first fin and a second fin that extend approximately perpendicular to the carrier module, and the first NFC coupler is positioned near a surface of the first fin, and the second NFC coupler is positioned near a surface of the second fin. 20. The method of claim 15, wherein the carrier module is a backplane.
